Auto Scaling.definition: Automatically add/remove EC2 based on demand

RPO.definition: Recovery Point Objective — max acceptable data loss measured in time

Throughput.definition: Amount of data transferred per second (MiB/s)

Security Group.definition: Stateful firewall at instance level — allows only

Multi-AZ.definition: Synchronous replication across 2+ AZs for high availability

Read Replica.definition: Asynchronous copy of DB for read scaling — NOT for HA

VPC Endpoint.definition: Private connection to AWS services without internet

Spot Instance.definition: Unused EC2 capacity at up to 90% discount — can be interrupted

IOPS.definition: Input/Output Operations Per Second — storage performance metric

Health Check.definition: ELB or Route 53 verifies endpoint is reachable

TTL.definition: Time To Live — auto-expire items in DynamoDB

Reserved Instance.definition: 1 or 3 year commitment for up to 75% discount

Signed URL.definition: Temporary URL granting access to private CloudFront/S3 content

Provisioned IOPS.definition: io1/io2 EBS — guaranteed IOPS for databases

MFA.definition: Multi-Factor Authentication — extra layer of security

Cross-Region Replication.definition: S3 automatically copies objects to another region

Target Tracking.definition: Scale to maintain a specific metric value (e.g. 70% CPU)

Scheduled Scaling.definition: Scale at specific times for predictable load

Visibility Timeout.definition: Time a message is hidden after being received from SQS

Step Scaling.definition: Scale in steps based on CloudWatch alarm thresholds

Stateless.definition: Application stores no session data on the server

POSIX.definition: Unix-style file system interface — required for EFS

Encryption at Rest.definition: Data encrypted when stored on disk

NACL.definition: Stateless firewall at subnet level — allows and denies

Cluster Placement Group.definition: Pack instances together for low-latency HPC

RTO.definition: Recovery Time Objective — max acceptable downtime after a failure

Ephemeral.definition: Temporary storage — lost when instance stops (Instance Store)

Savings Plan.definition: Flexible pricing model — commit to $/hr spend

Long Polling.definition: SQS waits for messages before responding — reduces empty polls

Origin Access Control.definition: Restricts S3 to only serve via CloudFront (OAC)

Enhanced Networking.definition: Higher bandwidth, lower latency for EC2

S3 Lifecycle Policy.definition: Automatically transition objects between storage classes

Sticky Session.definition: ELB routes user to same backend instance

Cross-Zone Load Balancing.definition: Distribute traffic evenly across all AZs

SAML 2.0.definition: Standard for identity federation with external IdP

Warm Standby.definition: DR strategy: scaled-down copy always running

Strong Consistency.definition: Read always returns most recent write

Dead Letter Queue.definition: SQS queue for failed messages after max retries

Pilot Light.definition: DR strategy: minimal core services always running

gp3.definition: Latest gen SSD EBS — cost-effective, configurable IOPS

Federated Identity.definition: Using external credentials to access AWS

Encryption in Transit.definition: Data encrypted while moving over network (SSL/TLS)

Blue/Green Deployment.definition: Run two environments, switch traffic instantly

Idempotent.definition: Operation that produces same result no matter how many times executed

Eventual Consistency.definition: Data will be consistent eventually — not immediately

Canary Deployment.definition: Route small % of traffic to new version first
